title: gouania microcarpa var. novo-hibernica
intro: Gouania microcarpa var. novo-hibernica, also known as the New South Wales Beebrush or New South Wales Gouania, is a plant species in the family Rhamnaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gouania microcarpa var. novo-hibernica include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
